{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[burial mound]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[burial mound]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[burial mound]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[burial mound]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[burial mound]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[burial mound]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide

==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide

==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide

It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. {{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Ojiyama Kofun Group (Otsu)}} *[https://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3012.pdf Shiga Prefecture Department of Education] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/970/ Shiga-Biwako Visitor's Guide] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/070.html Otsu City Museum of history]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[Lake Biwa]], west of [[Keihan Electric Railway]] [[Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide
