File:Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.02855|N|135.850675|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Ōtsu, Shiga|Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}}| architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]] [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], west of [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ōmijingūmae Station|Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[:en:Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Ōtsu Palace|Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōmijingūmae Station|Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[:en:Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide

{{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}}| architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]] [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], west of [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ōmijingūmae Station|Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[:en:Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Ōtsu Palace|Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōmijingūmae Station|Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[:en:Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Ojiyama Kofun Group (Otsu)}} *[https://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3012.pdf Shiga Prefecture Department of Education] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/970/ Shiga-Biwako Visitor's Guide] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/070.html Otsu City Museum of history]{{in lang|ja}}
Ōjiyama Kofun

It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[:en:Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Ōtsu Palace|Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōmijingūmae Station|Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[:en:Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide

The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ojiyama Kofun Group (Otsu)]][[en:Ōjiyama Kofun]][[ja:皇子山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30928191]] Wikidata name: Ōjiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image =Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg; File:Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.02855|N|135.850675|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Ōtsu, Shiga|Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}}| architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]] [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], west of [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ōmijingūmae Station|Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[:en:Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Ōtsu Palace|Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōmijingūmae Station|Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[:en:Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide

File:Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.02855|N|135.850675|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Ōtsu, Shiga|Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}}| architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]] [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], west of [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ōmijingūmae Station|Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[:en:Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Ōtsu Palace|Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōmijingūmae Station|Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[:en:Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Ojiyama Kofun Group (Otsu)}} *[https://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3012.pdf Shiga Prefecture Department of Education] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/970/ Shiga-Biwako Visitor's Guide] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/070.html Otsu City Museum of history]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Coord|35.02855|N|135.850675|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Ōtsu, Shiga|Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}}| architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]] [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], west of [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ōmijingūmae Station|Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[:en:Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Ōtsu Palace|Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōmijingūmae Station|Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[:en:Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]

{{Coord|35.02855|N|135.850675|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Ōtsu, Shiga|Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}}| architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]] [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], west of [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] [[:en:Ōmijingūmae Station|Ōmijingūmae Station]], at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful [[:en:Wani (scholar)|Wani clan]], which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] [[Ōmi Ōtsu Palace|Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from [[:en:Ōmijingūmae Station|Ōmijingūmae Station]] on the Keihan Electric Railway [[:en:Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Ojiyama Kofun Group (Otsu)}} *[https://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3012.pdf Shiga Prefecture Department of Education] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/970/ Shiga-Biwako Visitor's Guide] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/070.html Otsu City Museum of history]{{in lang|ja}}

File:Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.02855|N|135.850675|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Ōtsu, Shiga|Ōtsu, Shiga]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}; {{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907}}| region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}}| architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ōtsu|en|Ōtsu|WD=Q202907|lt=Ōtsu}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa}}, west of {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway}} {{ill|Ōmijingūmae Station|en|Ōmijingūmae Station|WD=Q8081616|lt=Ōmijingūmae Station}}, at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful {{ill|Wani (scholar)|en|Wani (scholar)|WD=Q482722|lt=Wani clan}}, which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} [[Ōmi Ōtsu Palace|Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from {{ill|Ōmijingūmae Station|en|Ōmijingūmae Station|WD=Q8081616|lt=Ōmijingūmae Station}} on the Keihan Electric Railway [[:en:Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Ojiyama Kofun Group (Otsu)}} *[https://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3012.pdf Shiga Prefecture Department of Education] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/970/ Shiga-Biwako Visitor's Guide] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/070.html Otsu City Museum of history]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image =Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg; File:Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.02855|N|135.850675|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Ōtsu, Shiga|Ōtsu, Shiga]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}; {{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907}}| region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}}| architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ōtsu|en|Ōtsu|WD=Q202907|lt=Ōtsu}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa}}, west of {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway}} {{ill|Ōmijingūmae Station|en|Ōmijingūmae Station|WD=Q8081616|lt=Ōmijingūmae Station}}, at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful {{ill|Wani (scholar)|en|Wani (scholar)|WD=Q482722|lt=Wani clan}}, which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} [[Ōmi Ōtsu Palace|Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from {{ill|Ōmijingūmae Station|en|Ōmijingūmae Station|WD=Q8081616|lt=Ōmijingūmae Station}} on the Keihan Electric Railway [[:en:Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Ojiyama Kofun Group (Otsu)}} *[https://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3012.pdf Shiga Prefecture Department of Education] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/970/ Shiga-Biwako Visitor's Guide] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/070.html Otsu City Museum of history]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image =Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg; File:Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.02855|N|135.850675|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Ōtsu, Shiga|Ōtsu, Shiga]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}| region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}| architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ōtsu|en|Ōtsu|WD=Q202907|lt=Ōtsu|1=Ōtsu}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa|1=Lake Biwa}}, west of {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|1=Keihan Electric Railway}} {{ill|Ōmijingūmae Station|en|Ōmijingūmae Station|WD=Q8081616|lt=Ōmijingūmae Station|1=Ōmijingūmae Station}}, at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful {{ill|Wani (scholar)|en|Wani (scholar)|WD=Q482722|lt=Wani clan|1=Wani (scholar)}}, which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} [[Ōmi Ōtsu Palace|Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from {{ill|Ōmijingūmae Station|en|Ōmijingūmae Station|WD=Q8081616|lt=Ōmijingūmae Station|1=Ōmijingūmae Station}} on the Keihan Electric Railway [[:en:Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ōjiyama Kofun | native_name = 皇子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image =Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg; File:Ojiyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.02855|N|135.850675|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Ōtsu, Shiga|Ōtsu, Shiga]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Otsu|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}| region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}| architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōjiyama Kofun'''|皇子山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nishikori neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ōtsu|en|Ōtsu|WD=Q202907|lt=Ōtsu|1=Ōtsu}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138847|title=皇子山古墳|trans-title= Ōjiyama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōjiyama Kofun is located in the southern part of the west bank of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa|1=Lake Biwa}}, west of {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|1=Keihan Electric Railway}} {{ill|Ōmijingūmae Station|en|Ōmijingūmae Station|WD=Q8081616|lt=Ōmijingūmae Station|1=Ōmijingūmae Station}}, at an elevation of 164 meters. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumulus ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), orientated to the south. it is covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' only on the side facing Lake Biwa, and there is evidence that it was designed to be visible from the lake. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1964 and 1970, at which time four burial facilities were found in the anterior portion and one in the posterior portion; however, a detailed investigation was not made of the burial chambers and grave good were not recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, it is the oldest tumulus to have been found in the Ōmi region. It is not known who built this tumulus, but it is believed to have some connection with the powerful {{ill|Wani (scholar)|en|Wani (scholar)|WD=Q482722|lt=Wani clan|1=Wani (scholar)}}, which is known to have ruled this area of Ōmi during the Kofun period. The location of the tumulus near the site of the late {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} [[Ōmi Ōtsu Palace|Ōmi Palace]] may also have some significance.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 1981 to 1985, the tumulus was restored, and is now part of an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] open to the public. It is located about ten minutes on foot from {{ill|Ōmijingūmae Station|en|Ōmijingūmae Station|WD=Q8081616|lt=Ōmijingūmae Station|1=Ōmijingūmae Station}} on the Keihan Electric Railway [[:en:Ishiyama Sakamoto Line|Ishiyama Sakamoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The National Historic Site designation includes a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of about 20 meters, which is slightly east of the main tumulus. The mound was once surrounded by a moat. It is estimated to date from around the end of the 3rd century AD, or earlier than the main tumulus.
;Overall length: 60 meters ;Posterior portion: 35 meters wide ;Anterior portion: 25 meters wide
